mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price inflation directly increases nominal yields as investors demand compensation for eroding purchasing power; this effect is persistent and always active across all regimes.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises but 10y yield declines over subsequent 20 trading days in more than 50% of instances, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the market expects the Fed to cut rates within months, causing long-term yields to decline in anticipation. This threshold-triggered mechanism is active only when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical pattern of yield decline after rate peak
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS >4.5% and 10y yield does not decline in the next 20 days (positive or flat) in >40% of such instances, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising industrial production and falling unemployment signal accelerating economic growth, which raises real rate expectations and pushes yields higher. The interaction of both proxies is required; each alone may not produce the same signal.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously but yields do not rise over next 60 trading days in >50% of occurrences, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4.0%), a rise in housing starts indicates capacity constraints and residential investment demand, leading to higher yields. Outside this regime, the signal is weaker or absent.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead yields in tight labor markets
failure_condition: If UNRATE <4.0% and HOUST rises but yields do not rise in next 20 days in >55% of cases, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal labor market strength and income growth, which supports consumption and inflation expectations, driving nominal yields upward. This effect is persistent across all regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls are key growth indicator for bond market
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises month-over-month and yields fall over next 5 trading days in more than 40% of cases, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL > 0.5% month-over-month) triggers inflation scare, causing immediate upward pressure on yields as markets price in tighter Fed policy.
evidence_refs: fact:High CPI prints cause yield spikes
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises >0.5% in a month and yields do not rise in next 5 days in >45% of events, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts and retail sales rise simultaneously, it signals robust domestic demand, boosting GDP expectations and driving yields up over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month but yields do not rise over next 60 trading days in >50% of instances, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an industrial recession, leading to flight-to-quality and lower yields.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial recession often precedes yield decline
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines two months in a row and yields do not decline in next 5 trading days in >40% of such episodes, hypothesis is rejected.
